spec_match.rationale: The concept now demonstrates the ChromeStatus contract for declarative partial HTML updates using named processing-instruction ranges and matching template patches.

interactive.rationale: The page has real delay controls, dispatch-mode toggles, play/reset controls, live queue status, metrics, and a document preview that changes as patches apply.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept and parent feature copy now both describe out-of-order HTML patch application rather than a generic ReadableStream reader behavior.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo surfaces queued chunks, repeated marker-style appends, missing target behavior, nested range ordering, and unsupported-browser fallback messaging.

references.rationale: The page links to the correct ChromeStatus entry, the declarative partial updates explainer, the WHATWG HTML pull request, and ProcessingInstruction reference material.

design_system.rationale: The touched concept uses shared CSS variables, explicit button foreground/background pairs, no inline styles or inline event handlers, and a responsive layout.

summary: Resolved the static-pipeline critique by rebuilding Chunk Ordering as an interactive declarative HTML patch scheduler aligned to the actual Chrome 150 feature.
